description: The thesaurus-shifted mood-conviction memo (eval-harness review 2026-07 finding #10): the mood_conviction thesis rewritten one hedge at a time into the highest-frequency LLM hedging vocabulary (potentially / looks / suggests / probably / presumably). The pre-fix hedge lexicon counted ZERO hedges here and passed the HIGH label; the extended pinned lexicon must count five and FAIL rubric conformance — the pin that keeps the hedge lexicon covering the center of the distribution it guards.

memo.thesis: This is potentially the moment the trend finally pays: the tape looks strong, the setup suggests durability, and the market is probably still underpricing the story, though the evidence is presumably thin beyond price itself.
kill_criteria: The close falls below the SMA50 and the twenty-session return turns negative
AVGO misses its next quarterly earnings report and guidance is cut
memo.dissent: Nothing in the quant record supports deployment: momentum-v1 failed its gates outright, the validated family declines to select the name, and buying on tape-feel alone is exactly the unaccountable discretion the constitution exists to prevent.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.
DCP indicators for AVGO as of 2026-07-10: SMA20 168.40, SMA50 161.75, 20-day return 8.86 percent, last close 172.35.
